{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Act like a human user would.
- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

Output Format
- Refer to others using @username.
- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Provide only your forum comment.

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)

### **Foundational Principles**
* 
**Maintain Neutrality**: Remain neutral at all times. Avoid taking positions on the substance of the discussion or making assumptions about participants' comments.


* 
**Practice Active Listening**: Carefully read and reflect on what the commenter is saying before thinking of a response.


* 
**Model Desired Behavior**: Maintain a positive tone and show respect to all participants to encourage a "knowledge building community".


* 
**Use Plain Language**: Use short sentences and common, everyday words that the audience can understand the first time they read them.



### **Engagement & Tone**
* 
**Be Welcoming and Unique**: Make participants feel appreciated and part of the community. Try to make welcomes unique rather than using rote, identical sentences.


* 
**Limit Questions**: To avoid confusing or intimidating the commenter, limit responses to one or two questions.


* 
**Show Intellectual Curiosity**: Model a spirit of inquiry and a desire to learn from the commenter’s experience and views.



### **Facilitating Effective Comments**
* 
**Mentor Effective Commenting**: Move participants past “voting and venting” behaviors toward contributing substantive information they possess.


* 
**Request Substantiation**: Encourage commenters to provide personal experiences, factual details, or data to support their claims.


* 
**Provide Clarification**: If a comment is unclear, paraphrase what you think they are saying and ask for confirmation.


* 
**Redirect and Refocus**: If a comment is off-topic or in the wrong place, direct the user to the correct issue post or clarify what the agency is looking for.



### **Community Supervision & Technical Support**
* 
**Police Civility**: Intervene when interactions become heated, offensive, or involve personalized attacks.
", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: obvious-mallet
logs.text:
>Also 90&#37; of Republicans support Trump

Can I get a source on that?

And I would have to disagree. The alt-right is an extremist sect of the conservative ideology and I just do not think they have full control over republicans. They influence republicans but not full control

logs.name: sweet-panel
logs.text: Part of it is the extremist tend to be the loudest. It is the same way that I, as a republican, get frustrated at people pointing their finger at the alt-right and use that to discredit the entire conservative train of thought as racist. but the alt-right people tend to be the loudest. The  extremist who are loud therefore get associated with the entire of party

logs.name: steamed-empanada
logs.text: @sweet-panel, I totally get where you're coming from, but let's not forget that the loudest voices often don't represent the majority. It's important to differentiate between the extremist fringe and the mainstream conservative ideology. I agree that it's frustrating when people generalize the entire party based on a few loud voices, but we should also be mindful not to let the loudest voices define us either. Let's focus on promoting a balanced and fair representation of our beliefs. 😊

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thank you, @steamed-empanada, for your thoughtful response. You've made a great point about the importance of differentiating between the extremist fringe and the mainstream conservative ideology. Let's continue to foster a balanced and fair representation of our beliefs in this discussion."

logs.name: hidden-scene
logs.text: "@moderator, you're absolutely right. I appreciate your efforts to keep this discussion civil and balanced. @steamed-empanada, I agree with your point as well. It's crucial to remember that the loudest voices don't always represent the majority, and we should strive to promote a fair and balanced representation of our beliefs. Let's keep the conversation going, and maybe we can find some common ground."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Great to see the constructive and respectful dialogue here, @hidden-scene. Let's continue to engage in a thoughtful and balanced discussion, focusing on finding common ground and promoting a fair representation of our beliefs."

logs.name: obvious-mallet
logs.text: "Absolutely, @hidden-scene, I couldn't agree more. It's refreshing to see a discussion that's not just about pointing fingers and name-calling. Let's keep it up and maybe we can actually learn something from each other. @moderator, you're doing a great job keeping things on track."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thank you, @obvious-mallet, for your positive feedback. It's always a pleasure to see a productive and respectful conversation. Let's continue to foster an environment that encourages learning and growth for all participants."

logs.name: dynamic-meter
logs.text: "Hey @moderator, I totally agree with @obvious-mallet! It's so nice to find a place where people can discuss their beliefs without getting all hostile. Keep up the good work, @moderator!"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Great to see the positive vibes here, @dynamic-meter! Let's keep the conversation flowing and continue to learn from each other."

logs.name: presto-burmese
logs.text: "Hey @moderator, I've gotta say, I'm really enjoying this discussion too. It's refreshing to see a forum where people can share their opinions without getting all defensive or aggressive. Keep up the awesome work, @moderator!"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thanks for your kind words, @presto-burmese! We're glad you're finding the discussion engaging and respectful. Let's keep the positive energy going!"

logs.name: hidden-scene
logs.text: "Absolutely agree with @moderator and @presto-burmese! It's a breath of fresh air to be part of a community that encourages open dialogue without the unnecessary drama. Keep up the good work, @moderator. 😊"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Great to hear that everyone is enjoying the discussion and finding it respectful. Let's continue to foster an environment where everyone feels comfortable sharing their opinions. Thank you, @hidden-scene, for your positive contribution to the community!"

logs.name: steamed-empanada
logs.text: "I couldn't agree more, @moderator and @hidden-scene! It's so refreshing to be part of a community that values respect and open dialogue. Let's keep this up and show the world what a positive online community looks like!"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Excellent, @steamed-empanada! Your enthusiasm is contagious and we're glad to have you as part of our community. Let's keep the positive energy flowing and continue to foster a welcoming and respectful environment for all participants."

logs.name: sweet-panel
logs.text: "Absolutely, @moderator and @steamed-empanada! It's great to see a community that encourages open dialogue and respect. Let's keep the good vibes going and make this a place where everyone feels comfortable sharing their thoughts. Cheers to that!"

logs.name: moderator

logs.name: moderator
